title: Sour Dense Algerian Egg Tagine
description: A midnightcraving Algerian tagine that blends the tang of preserved lemons with rich, sousvade eggs and a hearty, plantfriendly base, perfect for a dense, comforting dinner.
instructions: Preheat sous‑vide water bath to 80 °C (176 °F). Meanwhile, prepare the tagine.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a tagine or heavy‑bottom pot over medium heat. Sauté diced eggplant until softened, about 5 min.
Add tomato paste and spices (cumin, coriander, chili flakes). Stir for 1 min to release aromas.
Pour in oat milk and water, bring to a gentle simmer, then add chickpeas and half‑rinsed preserved lemon halves (cut into wedges). Cook for 5 min to meld flavors.
Peel and separate the preserved lemon into pulp and peel; reserve the pulp for later.
Place the eggs in a vacuum‑sealed bag or zip‑lock bag with a pinch of salt; seal and submerge in the preheated water bath. Cook 30 min, then remove and gently crack into the simmering tagine.
Add the pitted olives and the reserved lemon pulp; stir to combine. Cook for another 7 min, until the eggs are set but still creamy.
Remove from heat, drizzle remaining 1 tbsp olive oil over the tagine, and sprinkle fresh parsley.
Serve hot straight from the tagine, accompanied by crusty bread or warm couscous.
Optional: garnish with additional parsley or a squeeze of fresh lemon if desired.
